metadata.name: pyxsim
metadata.summary: Python package for simulating X-ray observations from astrophysical sources

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: doc.source.conf
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
pyxsim documentation build configuration file, created by
sphinx-quickstart on Sat Apr  9 10:00:48 2016.
This file is execfile()d with the current directory set to its
containing dir.
Note that not all possible configuration values are present in this
autogenerated file.
All configuration values have a default; values that are commented out
serve to show the default.

functions.name: pyxsim.utils.merge_files
documentation:
Helper function for merging PhotonList or EventList HDF5 files.

# Parameters

input_files

: List of filenames that will be merged together.

output_file

: Name of the merged file to be outputted.

overwrite

: If the output file already exists, set this to True to
  overwrite it.

add_exposure_times

: If set to True, exposure times will be added together. Otherwise,
  the exposure times of all of the files must be the same.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyxsim import merge_files
>>> merge_files(["events_0.h5","events_1.h5","events_3.h5"], "events.h5",
...             overwrite=True, add_exposure_times=True)
```

# Notes

Currently, to merge files it is mandated that all of the parameters have the
same values, with the exception of the exposure time parameter "exp_time". If
add_exposure_times=False, the maximum exposure time will be used.

functions.name: pyxsim.utils.create_metal_fields
documentation:
Create a set of metal abundance fields based on an abundance table
for a dataset that does not have them. An overall metallicity field
is required to scale the individual abundances by.

# Parameters

ds

: The dataset object for which this field will be created.

metallicity_field

: The metallicity field of the dataset.

elements

: The element or elements to make fields for.

abund_table

: The abundance table to use when computing the fields for the
  individual elements.

modules.name: pyxsim.event_list
documentation: Classes for generating lists of detected events

methods.name: pyxsim.event_list.EventList.__init__
documentation:
Read an EventList from disk so it can be exported to
other formats.

Parameters
----------
filespec : str
    A filename, list of filenames, or globbable path
    that yields a single or list of HDF5 files containing
    event data.

methods.name: pyxsim.event_list.EventList.write_fits_file
documentation:
Write events to a FITS binary table file. The result is an
"event file" which can be opened in ds9, binned into a
spectrum, etc., but NOTE that this does NOT represent an
actual observation since no instrumental effects are
included.

# Parameters

fitsfile

: The name of the event file to write.

fov

: The field of view of the event file. If units are not
  provided, they are assumed to be in arcminutes.

nx

: The resolution of the image (number of pixels on a side).

overwrite

: Set to True to overwrite a previous file.

methods.name: pyxsim.event_list.EventList.write_to_simput
documentation:
Write events to a SIMPUT catalog that may be utilized by various
instrument simulators.

Parameters
----------
prefix : string
    The filename prefix. The files to be written have the
    signature:
    f"{prefix}_simput.fits", f"{prefix}_phlist.fits", etc.
overwrite : boolean, optional
    Set to True to overwrite previous files.

methods.name: pyxsim.event_list.EventList.write_fits_image
documentation:
Generate an image by binning X-ray counts and write it to a FITS file.

# Parameters

imagefile

: The name of the image file to write.

fov

: The field of view of the image. If units are not provided, they
  are assumed to be in arcminutes.

nx

: The resolution of the image (number of pixels on a side).

emin

: The minimum energy of the photons to put in the image, in keV.

emax

: The maximum energy of the photons to put in the image, in keV.

overwrite

: Set to True to overwrite a previous file.

methods.name: pyxsim.event_list.EventList.write_spectrum
documentation:
Bin observer-frame event energies into a spectrum and write it to
a FITS binary table. This is for an *unconvolved* spectrum.

Parameters
----------
specfile : string
    The name of the FITS file to be written.
emin : float
    The minimum energy of the spectral bins in keV.
emax : float
    The maximum energy of the spectral bins in keV.
nchan : integer
    The number of channels.
overwrite : boolean, optional
    Set to True to overwrite a previous file.

functions.name: pyxsim.xray_binaries.make_xrb_particles
documentation:
This routine generates an in-memory dataset composed of X-ray binary particles
from an input data source containing star particles.

# Parameters

data_source

: The yt data source to obtain the data from, such as a sphere, box, disk,
  etc.

age_field

: The stellar age field. Must be in some kind of time units.

scale_length

: The radial length scale over which to scatter the XRB particles
  from their parent star particle. Can be the name of a smoothing
  length field for the stars, a (value, unit) tuple, or a YTQuantity.

sfr_time_range

: The recent time range over which to calculate the star formation rate from
  the current time in the dataset. Default: 1.0 Gyr

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers, such as for a
  test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

functions.name: pyxsim.xray_binaries.make_xrb_photons
documentation:
Take a dataset produced by
{func}`~pyxsim.source_generators.xray_binaries.make_xrb_particles`
and produce a {class}`~pyxsim.photon_list.PhotonList`.

# Parameters

photon_prefix

: The prefix of the filename(s) containing the photon list. If run in
  serial, the filename will be "\{photon_prefix}.h5", if run in
  parallel, the filenames will be "\{photon_prefix}.\{mpi_rank}.h5".

ds

: The dataset of XRB particles to use to make the photons.

redshift

: The cosmological redshift for the photons.

area

: The collecting area to determine the number of photons. If units are
  not specified, it is assumed to be in cm^2.

exp_time

: The exposure time to determine the number of photons. If units are
  not specified, it is assumed to be in seconds.

emin

: The minimum energy of the photons to be generated, in the rest frame of
  the source. If units are not given, they are assumed to be in keV.

emax

: The maximum energy of the photons to be generated, in the rest frame of
  the source. If units are not given, they are assumed to be in keV.

center

: The origin of the photon spatial coordinates. Accepts "c", "max", or a coordinate.
  If not specified, pyxsim attempts to use the "center" field parameter of the data_source.

cosmology

: Cosmological information. If not supplied, we try to get
  the cosmology from the dataset. Otherwise, LCDM with
  the default yt parameters is assumed.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers, such as for a
  test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

classes.name: pyxsim.source_models.line_sources.LineSourceModel

methods.name: pyxsim.source_models.line_sources.LineSourceModel.make_spectrum
documentation:
Make a count rate spectrum in the source frame from a yt data container,
or a spectrum in the observer frame.

# Parameters

data_source

: The data source from which the photons will be generated.

emin

: The minimum energy in the band. If a float, it is assumed to be
  in keV.

emax

: The minimum energy in the band. If a float, it is assumed to be
  in keV.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectrum.

redshift

: If greater than 0, we assume that the spectrum should be created in
  the observer frame at a distance given by the cosmology. Default: 0.0

dist

: The distance to a nearby source, if redshift = 0.0. If a float, it
  is assumed to be in units of kpc.

cosmology

: Cosmological information. If not supplied, we try to get the
  cosmology from the dataset. Otherwise, LCDM with the default yt
  parameters is assumed.

# Returns

{class}`~soxs.spectra.CountRateSpectrum` or {class}`~soxs.spectra.Spectrum`,
depending on how the method is invoked.

Initialize a source model from a single line.

# Parameters

e0

: The location of the emission line in energy in the rest frame of the
  source. If units are not given, they are assumed to be in keV.

emission_field

: The field corresponding to the photon count rate per cell or particle,
  in the rest frame of the source, which serves as the normalization for
  the line. Must be in counts/s.

sigma

: The standard intrinsic deviation of the emission line (not from Doppler
  broadening, which is handled in the projection step). Units of
  velocity or energy are accepted. If units are not given, they
  are assumed to be in keV. If set to a field name, the line broadening
  is assumed to be based on this field (in units of velocity or energy).
  If set to None (the default), it is assumed that the line is unbroadened.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers, such as for a
  test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> location = (3.5, "keV")
>>> sigma = (1000., "km/s")
>>> line_model = LineSourceModel(location, "dark_matter_density_squared", sigma=sigma)
```

modules.name: pyxsim.spectral_models
documentation: Photon emission and absoprtion models.

methods.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.ThermalSpectralModel.get_spectrum
documentation: Get the thermal emission spectrum given a temperature *kT* in keV.

classes.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.TableCIEModel

methods.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.TableCIEModel.prepare_spectrum
documentation: Prepare the thermal model for execution given a redshift *zobs* for the spectrum.

Initialize a thermal gas emission model from the AtomDB APEC tables
available at <http://www.atomdb.org>. This code borrows heavily from Python
routines used to read the APEC tables developed by Adam Foster at the
CfA (<mailto:afoster@cfa.harvard.edu>).

# Parameters

emin

: The minimum energy for the spectral model.

emax

: The maximum energy for the spectral model.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectral model. If one
  is thermally broadening lines, it is recommended that
  this value result in an energy resolution per channel
  of roughly 1 eV or smaller.

binscale

: The scale of the energy binning: "linear" or "log".
  Default: "linear"

var_elem

: The names of elements to allow to vary freely
  from the single abundance parameter. Default:
  None

model_root

: The directory root where the model files are stored. If
  not provided, the default SOXS-provided files are used.

model_vers

: The version identifier string for the APEC files, e.g.
  "3.0.3". Default: 3.0.9

thermal_broad

: Whether the spectral lines should be thermally
  broadened. Default: True

nolines

: Turn off lines entirely for generating spectra.
  Default: False

abund_table

: The abundance table to be used for solar abundances.
  Either a string corresponding to a built-in table or an array
  of 30 floats corresponding to the abundances of each element
  relative to the abundance of H. Default is "angr".
  Built-in options are:
  "angr" : from Anders E. & Grevesse N. (1989, Geochimica et
  Cosmochimica Acta 53, 197)
  "aspl" : from Asplund M., Grevesse N., Sauval A.J. & Scott
  P. (2009, ARAA, 47, 481)
  "wilm" : from Wilms, Allen & McCray (2000, ApJ 542, 914
  except for elements not listed which are given zero abundance)
  "lodd" : from Lodders, K (2003, ApJ 591, 1220)

nei

: If True, use the non-equilibrium ionization tables. Only available
  for the "apec" model. Default: False

# Examples

```pycon
>>> apec_model = TableCIEModel("apec", 0.05, 50.0, 1000, model_vers="3.0.3",
...                            thermal_broad=False)
```

methods.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.IGMSpectralModel.prepare_spectrum
documentation: Prepare the thermal model for execution given a redshift *zobs* for the spectrum.

class_variables.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.IGMSpectralModel._logT
documentation:
A spectral model for a thermal plasma including photoionization and
resonant scattering from the CXB based on Khabibullin & Churazov 2019
(<https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2019MNRAS.482.4972K/>) and Churazov
et al. 2001 (<https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2001MNRAS.323...93C/>).

For temperatures higher than kT ~ 1.09 keV, a Cloudy-based CIE model
is used to compute the spectrum.

Assumes the abundance tables from Feldman 1992.

Table data and README files can be found at
<https://wwwmpa.mpa-garching.mpg.de/~ildar/igm/v2x/>.

# Parameters

emin

: The minimum energy for the spectral model.

emax

: The maximum energy for the spectral model.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectral model.

resonant_scattering

: Whether or not to include the effects of resonant scattering
  from CXB photons. Default: False

cxb_factor

: The fraction of the CXB photons that are resonant scattered to enhance
  the lines. Default: 0.5

var_elem

: The names of elements to allow to vary freely
  from the single abundance parameter. Default:
  None

methods.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.AbsorptionModel.get_absorb
documentation: Get the absorption spectrum.

methods.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.AbsorptionModel.absorb_photons
documentation:
Determine which photons will be absorbed by foreground
galactic absorption.

# Parameters

eobs

: The energies of the photons in keV.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers, such as for a
  test. Default is the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

classes.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.TBabsModel
documentation:
Initialize a Tuebingen-Boulder (Wilms, J., Allen, A., &
McCray, R. 2000, ApJ, 542, 914) ISM absorption model.

# Parameters

nH

: The foreground column density in units of 10^22 cm^\{-2}.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> tbabs_model = TBabsModel(0.1)
```

classes.name: pyxsim.spectral_models.WabsModel
documentation:
Initialize a Wisconsin (Morrison and McCammon; ApJ 270, 119)
absorption model.

# Parameters

nH

: The foreground column density in units of 10^22 cm^\{-2}.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> wabs_model = WabsModel(0.1)
```

classes.name: pyxsim.source_models.power_law_sources.PowerLawSourceModel

methods.name: pyxsim.source_models.power_law_sources.PowerLawSourceModel.make_spectrum
documentation:
Make a count rate spectrum in the source frame from a yt data container,
or a spectrum in the observer frame.

# Parameters

data_source

: The data source from which the photons will be generated.

emin

: The minimum energy in the band. If a float, it is assumed to be
  in keV.

emax

: The minimum energy in the band. If a float, it is assumed to be
  in keV.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectrum.

redshift

: If greater than 0, we assume that the spectrum should be created in
  the observer frame at a distance given by the cosmology. Default: 0.0

dist

: The distance to a nearby source, if redshift = 0.0. If a float, it
  is assumed to be in units of kpc.

cosmology

: Cosmological information. If not supplied, we try to get the
  cosmology from the dataset. Otherwise, LCDM with the default yt
  parameters is assumed.

# Returns

{class}`~soxs.spectra.CountRateSpectrum` or {class}`~soxs.spectra.Spectrum`,
depending on how the method is invoked.

Initialize a source model from a power-law spectrum.

# Parameters

e0

: The reference energy of the power law, in the rest frame of the source.
  If units are not given, they are assumed to be in keV.

emin

: The minimum energy of the photons to be generated, in the rest frame of
  the source. If units are not given, they are assumed to be in keV.

emax

: The maximum energy of the photons to be generated, in the rest frame of
  the source. If units are not given, they are assumed to be in keV.

emission_field

: The field corresponding to the specific photon count rate per cell or
  particle, in the rest frame of the source, which serves as the
  normalization for the power law. Must be in counts/s/keV.

index

: The power-law index of the spectrum. Either a float for a single power law or
  the name of a field that corresponds to the power law.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers, such as for a
  test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> e0 = (1.0, "keV")
>>> emin = (0.01, "keV")
>>> emax = (100., "keV")
>>> plaw_model = PowerLawSourceModel(e0, emin, emax, ("gas", "norm"), ("gas", "index"))
```

methods.name: pyxsim.source_models.sources.SourceModel.make_source_fields
documentation:
Make the following fields in the rest frame of the
source within a specific energy band for a dataset in yt:

f"xray_emissivity\_\{emin}\_\{emax}\_keV" (in erg/cm\*\*3/s)
f"xray_luminosity\_\{emin}\_\{emax}\_keV" (in erg/s)
f"xray_photon_emissivity\_\{emin}\_\{emax}\_keV" (in photons/cm\*\*3/s)
f"xray_count_rate\_\{emin}\_\{emax}\_keV" (in photons/s)

where "emin" and "emax" are the bounds of the energy band
as described below. In this case, emin and emax are the bounds
of the energy in the source frame.

# Parameters

ds

: The loaded yt dataset to make the fields for.

emin

: The minimum energy in the band in the source frame.
  If a float, it is assumed to be in keV.

emax

: The minimum energy in the band in the source frame.
  If a float, it is assumed to be in keV.

force_override

: If True, override a pre-existing field with the same name.
  Default: False

band_name

: The name to give to the energy band in the field. If None,
  it is set to "\{emin}\_\{emax}\_keV". Default: None

# Returns

The list of fields which are generated.

methods.name: pyxsim.source_models.sources.SourceModel.make_line_source_fields
documentation:
Make a list of source fields for a very small bandpass,
essentially covering a line. The fields are of the form:

f"xray_emissivity\_\{line_name}" (in erg/cm\*\*3/s)
f"xray_luminosity\_\{line_name}" (in erg/s)
f"xray_photon_emissivity\_\{line_name}" (in photons/cm\*\*3/s)
f"xray_count_rate\_\{emin}\_\{line_name}" (in photons/s)

In this case, e0 and de are in the source frame.

# Parameters

ds

: The loaded yt dataset to make the fields for.

e0

: The line centroid in the source frame. If a float, it is
  assumed to be in keV.

de

: The width of the band in the source frame. If a float, it is
  assumed to be in keV.

band_name

: The suffix of the field name for the line. Logical names are
  "O_VII", "O_VIII", "Ne_IX", etc.

force_override

: If True, override a pre-existing field with the same name.
  Default: False

# Returns

The list of fields which are generated.

methods.name: pyxsim.source_models.sources.SourceModel.make_intensity_fields
documentation:
Make the following fields in the observer frame within a
specific energy band for a dataset in yt:

f"xray_intensity\_\{emin}\_\{emax}\_keV" (in erg/cm\*\*3/s/arcsec\*\*2)
f"xray_photon_intensity\_\{emin}\_\{emax}\_keV" (in photons/cm\*\*3/s/arcsec\*\*2)

where "emin" and "emax" are the bounds of the energy band as
described below. These should mainly be used for projections.
In this case, emin and emax are the bounds of the energy in the
observer frame.

# Parameters

ds

: The loaded yt dataset to make the fields for.

emin

: The minimum energy in the band in the observer frame. If a float,
  it is assumed to be in keV.

emax

: The maximum energy in the band in the observer frame. If a float,
  it is assumed to be in keV.

redshift

: The redshift of the source. Default: 0.0

dist

: The angular diameter distance, used for nearby sources. This may be
  optionally supplied instead of it being determined from the
  *redshift* and given *cosmology*. If units are not specified, it is
  assumed to be in kpc. To use this, the redshift must be set to zero.

cosmology

: Cosmological information. If not supplied, we try to get the
  cosmology from the dataset. Otherwise, LCDM with the default yt
  parameters is assumed.

force_override

: If True, override a pre-existing field with the same name.
  Default: False

band_name

: The name to give to the energy band in the field. If None,
  it is set to "\{emin}\_\{emax}\_keV". Default: None

# Returns

The list of fields which are generated.

methods.name: pyxsim.source_models.sources.SourceModel.make_line_intensity_fields
documentation:
Make a list of intensity fields for a very small bandpass,
essentially covering a line. The fields are of the form:

f"xray_intensity\_\{line_name}" (in erg/cm\*\*3/s/arcsec\*\*2)
f"xray_photon_intensity\_\{line_name}" (in photons/cm\*\*3/s/arcsec\*\*2)

These should mainly be used for projections. In this case, e0 and de
are in the observer frame.

# Parameters

ds

: The loaded yt dataset to make the fields for.

e0

: The line centroid in the observer frame. If a float, it is
  assumed to be in keV.

de

: The width of the band in the observer frame. If a float,
  it is assumed to be in keV.

band_name

: The suffix of the field name for the line. Logical names are
  "O_VII", "O_VIII", "Ne_IX", etc.

redshift

: The redshift of the source. Default: 0.0

dist

: The angular diameter distance, used for nearby sources. This may be
  optionally supplied instead of it being determined from the
  *redshift* and given *cosmology*. If units are not specified, it is
  assumed to be in kpc. To use this, the redshift must be set to zero.

cosmology

: Cosmological information. If not supplied, we try to get the
  cosmology from the dataset. Otherwise, LCDM with the default yt
  parameters is assumed.

force_override

: If True, override a pre-existing field with the same name.
  Default: False

# Returns

The list of fields which are generated.

modules.name: pyxsim.photon_list
documentation: Classes for generating lists of photons

functions.name: pyxsim.photon_list.find_object_bounds
documentation:
This logic is required to determine the bounds of the object, which is
solely for fixing coordinates at periodic boundaries

functions.name: pyxsim.photon_list.make_photons
documentation:
Write a photon list dataset to disk from a yt data source and assuming a
source model for the photons. The redshift, collecting area, exposure time,
and cosmology are stored in the *parameters* dictionary which is passed to
the *source_model* function.

# Parameters

photon_prefix

: The prefix of the filename(s) to contain the photon list. If run in
  serial, the filename will be "\{photon_prefix}.h5", if run in
  parallel, the filenames will be "\{photon_prefix}.\{mpi_rank}.h5".

data_source

: The data source from which the photons will be generated.

redshift

: The cosmological redshift for the photons.

area

: The collecting area to determine the number of photons. If units are
  not specified, it is assumed to be in cm^2.

exp_time

: The exposure time to determine the number of photons. If units are
  not specified, it is assumed to be in seconds.

source_model

: A source model used to generate the photons.

point_sources

: If True, the photons will be assumed to be generated from the exact
  positions of the cells or particles and not smeared around within
  a volume. Default: False

parameters

: A dictionary of parameters to be passed for the source model to use,
  if necessary.

center

: The origin of the photon spatial coordinates. Accepts "c", "max", or
  a coordinate. If array-like and without units, it is assumed to be in
  units of kpc. If not specified, pyxsim attempts to use the "center"
  field parameter of the data_source.

dist

: The angular diameter distance, used for nearby sources. This may be
  optionally supplied instead of it being determined from the
  *redshift* and given *cosmology*. If units are not specified, it is
  assumed to be in kpc. To use this, the redshift must be set to zero.

cosmology

: Cosmological information. If not supplied, we try to get the
  cosmology from the dataset. Otherwise, LCDM with the default yt
  parameters is assumed.

velocity_fields

: The yt fields to use for the velocity. If not specified, the
  following will be assumed:
  ['velocity_x', 'velocity_y', 'velocity_z'] for grid datasets
  ['particle_velocity_x', 'particle_velocity_y', 'particle_velocity_z'] for particle datasets

bulk_velocity

: A 3-element array or list specifying the local velocity frame of
  reference. If not a {class}`~yt.units.yt_array.YTArray`, it is assumed
  to have units of km/s. Default: [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] km/s.

observer

: Controls whether the source is at a large distance, in which case
  the observer sits far outside the source ("external"), or if the
  observer is inside the X-ray-emitting source ("internal"), such as a
  galaxy. Default: "external"

fields_to_keep

: A list of fields to add to the HDF5 photon list in addition to the cell
  or particle positions, velocities, and sizes. Default: None

# Returns

A tuple of two integers, the number of photons and the number of cells with
photons

# Examples

```pycon
>>> thermal_model = pyxsim.CIESourceModel("apec", 0.1, 10.0, 1000, 0.3)
>>> redshift = 0.05
>>> area = 6000.0 # assumed here in cm**2
>>> exp_time = 2.0e5 # assumed here in seconds
>>> sp = ds.sphere("c", (500., "kpc"))
>>> fields_to_keep = [("gas", "density"), ("gas", "temperature")]
>>> n_photons, n_cells = pyxsim.make_photons(
...     sp, redshift, area, exp_time, thermal_model,
...     fields_to_keep=fields_to_keep
... )
```

functions.name: pyxsim.photon_list.project_photons
documentation:
Projects photons onto an image plane given a line of sight, and
stores them in an HDF5 dataset which contains an event list.

# Parameters

photon_prefix

: The prefix of the filename(s) containing the photon list. If run in
  serial, the filename will be "\{photon_prefix}.h5", if run in
  parallel, the filenames will be "\{photon_prefix}.\{mpi_rank}.h5".

event_prefix

: The prefix of the filename(s) which will be written to contain the
  event list. If run in serial, the filename will be "\{event_prefix}.h5",
  if run in parallel, the filename will be "\{event_prefix}.\{mpi_rank}.h5".

normal

: Normal vector to the plane of projection. If "x", "y", or "z", will
  assume to be along that axis (and will probably be faster). Otherwise,
  should be an off-axis normal vector, e.g [1.0, 2.0, -3.0]

sky_center

: Center RA, Dec of the events in degrees.

absorb_model

: A model for foreground galactic absorption, to simulate the
  absorption of events before being detected. Known options for
  are "wabs" and "tbabs".

nH

: The foreground column density in units of 10^22 cm^\{-2}. Only used
  if absorption is applied.

abund_table

: The abundance table to be used for abundances in the
  absorption model (only used for TBabs). Default is set in the SOXS
  configuration file, the default for which is "angr".
  Built-in options are:
  "angr" : from Anders E. & Grevesse N. (1989, Geochimica et
  Cosmochimica Acta 53, 197)
  "aspl" : from Asplund M., Grevesse N., Sauval A.J. & Scott
  P. (2009, ARAA, 47, 481)
  "feld" : from Feldman U. (1992, Physica Scripta, 46, 202)
  "wilm" : from Wilms, Allen & McCray (2000, ApJ 542, 914
  except for elements not listed which are given zero abundance)
  "lodd" : from Lodders, K (2003, ApJ 591, 1220)
  "cl17.03" : the default abundance table in Cloudy 17.03

no_shifting

: If set, the photon energies will not be Doppler shifted. Default: False

north_vector

: A vector defining the "up" direction. This option sets the
  orientation of the plane of projection. If not set, an arbitrary
  grid-aligned north_vector perpendicular to the normal is chosen.
  Ignored in the case where a particular axis (e.g., "x", "y", or
  "z") is explicitly specified.

sigma_pos

: Apply a gaussian smoothing operation to the sky positions of the
  events. This may be useful when the binned events appear blocky due
  to their uniform distribution within simulation cells. However, this
  will move the events away from their originating position on the
  sky, and so may distort surface brightness profiles and/or spectra.
  Should probably only be used for visualization purposes. Supply a
  float here to smooth with a standard deviation with this fraction
  of the cell size. Default: None

flat_sky

: If set, we assume that the sky is "flat" and RA, Dec positions are
  computed using simple linear offsets

kernel

: The kernel used when smoothing positions of X-rays originating from
  SPH particles, "gaussian" or "top_hat". Default: "top_hat".

save_los

: If True, save the line-of-sight positions along the projection axis in
  units of kpc to the events list. Default: False

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers,
  such as for a test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random`
  module.

# Returns

A integer for the number of events created

# Examples

```pycon
>>> L = np.array([0.1,-0.2,0.3])
>>> n_events = pyxsim.project_photons("my_photons.h5", "my_events.h5", L,
...                                   [30., 45.], absorb_model='tbabs',
...                                   nH=0.04)
```

functions.name: pyxsim.photon_list.project_photons_allsky
documentation:
Projects photons onto the sky sphere given a normal vector ("z" or "up" in
spherical coordinates), and stores them in an HDF5 dataset which contains
an event list.

# Parameters

photon_prefix

: The prefix of the filename(s) containing the photon list. If run in
  serial, the filename will be "\{photon_prefix}.h5", if run in
  parallel, the filenames will be "\{photon_prefix}.\{mpi_rank}.h5".

event_prefix

: The prefix of the filename(s) which will be written to contain the
  event list. If run in serial, the filename will be "\{event_prefix}.h5",
  if run in parallel, the filename will be "\{event_prefix}.\{mpi_rank}.h5".

normal

: The vector determining the "z" or "up" vector for the spherical coordinate
  system for the all-sky projection, something like [1.0, 2.0, -3.0]. It
  will be normalized before use.

absorb_model

: A model for foreground galactic absorption, to simulate the
  absorption of events before being detected. Known options are "wabs"
  and "tbabs".

nH

: The foreground column density in units of 10^22 cm^\{-2}. Only used
  if absorption is applied.

abund_table

: The abundance table to be used for abundances in the
  absorption model (only used for TBabs). Default is set in the SOXS
  configuration file, the default for which is "angr".
  Built-in options are:
  "angr" : from Anders E. & Grevesse N. (1989, Geochimica et
  Cosmochimica Acta 53, 197)
  "aspl" : from Asplund M., Grevesse N., Sauval A.J. & Scott
  P. (2009, ARAA, 47, 481)
  "feld" : from Feldman U. (1992, Physica Scripta, 46, 202)
  "wilm" : from Wilms, Allen & McCray (2000, ApJ 542, 914
  except for elements not listed which are given zero abundance)
  "lodd" : from Lodders, K (2003, ApJ 591, 1220)
  "cl17.03" : the default abundance table in Cloudy 17.03

no_shifting

: If set, the photon energies will not be Doppler shifted. Default: False

center_vector

: A vector defining what direction will be placed at the center of
  the lat/lon coordinate system. If not set, an arbitrary
  grid-aligned center_vector perpendicular to the normal is chosen.

kernel

: The kernel used when smoothing positions of X-rays originating from
  SPH particles, "gaussian" or "top_hat". Default: "top_hat".

save_los

: If True, save the line-of-sight radii in units of kpc to the events
  file. Default: False

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers,
  such as for a test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random`
  module.

# Returns

A integer for the number of events created

# Examples

```pycon
>>> L = np.array([0.1,-0.2,0.3])
>>> n_events = pyxsim.project_photons_allsky("my_photons.h5", "my_events.h5", L)
```

methods.name: pyxsim.photon_list.PhotonList.__init__
documentation:
Read a PhotonList from disk to get information about it
or to export to other formats.

Parameters
----------
filespec : str
    A filename, list of filenames, or globbable path
    that yields a single or list of HDF5 files containing
    event data.

methods.name: pyxsim.photon_list.PhotonList.write_spectrum
documentation:
Bin photon energies into a spectrum and write it to a FITS binary
table. This is for an *unconvolved* spectrum.

Parameters
----------
specfile : string
    The name of the FITS file to be written.
emin : float
    The minimum energy of the spectral bins in keV.
emax : float
    The maximum energy of the spectral bins in keV.
nchan : integer
    The number of channels.
overwrite : boolean, optional
    Set to True to overwrite a previous file.

methods.name: pyxsim.light_cone.XrayLightCone.generate_events
documentation:
Generate projected events from a light cone simulation.

# Parameters

photon_prefix

: The prefix of the filename(s) containing the photon list. If run in
  serial, the filename will be "\{photon_prefix}.lc\{i}.h5", where i
  iterates over the elements of the light cone solution. If run in
  parallel, the filenames will be "\{photon_prefix}.lc\{i}.\{mpi_rank}.h5".

event_prefix

: The prefix of the filename(s) which will be written to contain the
  event list. If run in serial, the filename will be "\{event_prefix}.h5",
  if run in parallel, the filename will be "\{event_prefix}.\{mpi_rank}.h5".

area

: The collecting area to determine the number of events. If units are
  not specified, it is assumed to be in cm^2.

exp_time

: The exposure time to determine the number of events. If units are
  not specified, it is assumed to be in seconds.

angular_width

: The angular width of the light cone simulation. If units are not
  specified, it is assumed to be in degrees.

source_model

: A source model used to generate the events.

sky_center

: Center RA, Dec of the events in degrees.

parameters

: A dictionary of parameters to be passed for the source model to use,
  if necessary.

velocity_fields

: The yt fields to use for the velocity. If not specified, the following will
  be assumed:
  ['velocity_x', 'velocity_y', 'velocity_z'] for grid datasets
  ['particle_velocity_x', 'particle_velocity_y', 'particle_velocity_z'] for particle datasets

absorb_model

: A model for foreground galactic absorption, to simulate the absorption
  of events before being detected. Known options are "wabs" and "tbabs".

nH

: The foreground column density in units of 10^22 cm^\{-2}. Only used if
  absorption is applied.

no_shifting

: If set, the photon energies will not be Doppler shifted.

sigma_pos

: Apply a gaussian smoothing operation to the sky positions of the
  events. This may be useful when the binned events appear blocky due
  to their uniform distribution within simulation cells. However, this
  will move the events away from their originating position on the
  sky, and so may distort surface brightness profiles and/or spectra.
  Should probably only be used for visualization purposes. Supply a
  float here to smooth with a standard deviation with this fraction
  of the cell size. Default: None

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers, such as for a
  test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

methods.name: pyxsim.source_models.thermal_sources.ThermalSourceModel.make_spectrum
documentation:
Make a count rate spectrum in the source frame from a yt data container,
or a spectrum in the observer frame.

# Parameters

data_source

: The data source from which the photons will be generated.

emin

: The minimum energy in the band. If a float, it is assumed to be
  in keV.

emax

: The minimum energy in the band. If a float, it is assumed to be
  in keV.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectrum.

redshift

: If greater than 0, we assume that the spectrum should be created in
  the observer frame at a distance given by the cosmology. Default: 0.0

dist

: The distance to a nearby source, if redshift = 0.0. If a float, it
  is assumed to be in units of kpc.

cosmology

: Cosmological information. If not supplied, we try to get the
  cosmology from the dataset. Otherwise, LCDM with the default yt
  parameters is assumed.

# Returns

{class}`~soxs.spectra.CountRateSpectrum` or {class}`~soxs.spectra.Spectrum`,
depending on how the method is invoked.

classes.name: pyxsim.source_models.thermal_sources.IGMSourceModel
documentation:
A source model for a thermal plasma including photoionization and
resonant scattering from the CXB based on Khabibullin & Churazov 2019
(<https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2019MNRAS.482.4972K/>) and Churazov
et al. 2001 (<https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2001MNRAS.323...93C/>).

For temperatures higher than kT ~ 1.09 keV, a Cloudy-based CIE model
is used to compute the spectrum.

Assumes the abundance table from Feldman 1992.

Table data and README files can be found at
<https://wwwmpa.mpa-garching.mpg.de/~ildar/igm/v3/>.

# Parameters

emin

: The minimum energy for the spectral model.

emax

: The maximum energy for the spectral model.

nbins

: The number of bins in the spectral model. If one
  is thermally broadening lines, it is recommended that
  this value result in an energy resolution per channel
  of roughly 1 eV or smaller.

Zmet

: The metallicity. If a float, assumes a constant metallicity throughout
  in solar units. If a string or tuple of strings, is taken to be the
  name of the metallicity field.

binscale

: The scale of the energy binning: "linear" or "log".
  Default: "linear"

resonant_scattering

: Whether to include the effects of resonant scattering
  from CXB photons. Default: False

cxb_factor

: The fraction of the CXB photons that are resonant scattered to enhance
  the lines. Default: 0.5

model_vers

: The version identifier string for the model files, "3" or "4".

nh_field

: The yt hydrogen nuclei density field (meaning all hydrogen, ionized or not)
  to use for the model. Must have units of cm\*\*-3.
  Default: ("gas", "H_nuclei_density")

temperature_field

: The yt temperature field to use for the thermal modeling. Must have
  units of Kelvin. Default: ("gas", "temperature")

emission_measure_field

: The yt emission measure field to use for the thermal modeling. Must
  have units of cm^-3. Default: ("gas", "emission_measure")

h_fraction

: The hydrogen mass fraction. If a float, assumes a constant mass
  fraction of hydrogen throughout. If a string or tuple of strings,
  is taken to be the name of the hydrogen fraction field. Defaults to
  the appropriate value for the Feldman abundance table.

kT_min

: The default minimum temperature in keV to compute emission for.
  Default: 0.00431

kT_max

: The default maximum temperature in keV to compute emission for.
  Default: 64.0

max_density

: The maximum density of the cells or particles to use when generating
  photons. If a float, the units are assumed to be g/cm\*\*3.
  Default: None, meaning no maximum density.

min_entropy

: The minimum entropy of the cells or particles to use when generating
  photons. If a float, the units are assumed to be keV\*cm\*\*2.
  Default: None, meaning no minimum entropy.

var_elem

: Elements that should be allowed to vary freely from the single abundance
  parameter. Each dictionary value, specified by the abundance symbol,
  corresponds to the abundance of that symbol. If a float, it is understood
  to be constant and in solar units. If a string or tuple of strings, it is
  assumed to be a spatially varying field. Default: None

method

: The method used to generate the photon energies from the spectrum:
  "invert_cdf": Invert the cumulative distribution function of the spectrum.
  "accept_reject": Acceptance-rejection method using the spectrum.
  The first method should be sufficient for most cases.

model_vers

: The version of the IGM tables to use in the calculations.
  Options are:
  "4_lo": Tables computed from Cloudy using a continuum resolution
  of 0.1 with a range of 0.05 to 10 keV.
  "4_hi": Tables computed from Cloudy using enhanced continuum
  resolution of 0.025 with a range of 0.05 to 10 keV. Excellent
  energy resolution, but may be expensive to evaluate.
  Default: "4_lo"

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers,
  such as for a test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

classes.name: pyxsim.source_models.thermal_sources.CIESourceModel
documentation:
Initialize a source model from a CIE spectrum, using either
the APEC, SPEX, MeKaL, or Cloudy models.

# Parameters

model

: Which spectral emission model to use. Accepts either "apec", "spex",
  "mekal", or "cloudy".

emin

: The minimum energy for the spectrum in keV.

emax

: The maximum energy for the spectrum in keV.

nbins

: The number of channels in the spectrum.

Zmet

: The metallicity. If a float, assumes a constant metallicity throughout
  in solar units. If a string or tuple of strings, is taken to be the
  name of the metallicity field.

binscale

: The scale of the energy binning: "linear" or "log".
  Default: "linear"

temperature_field

: The yt temperature field to use for the thermal modeling. Must have
  units of Kelvin. Default: ("gas", "temperature")

emission_measure_field

: The yt emission measure field to use for the thermal modeling. Must
  have units of cm^-3. Default: ("gas", "emission_measure")

h_fraction

: The hydrogen mass fraction. If a float, assumes a constant mass
  fraction of hydrogen throughout. If a string or tuple of strings,
  is taken to be the name of the hydrogen fraction field. Default is
  whatever value is appropriate for the chosen abundance table.

kT_min

: The default minimum temperature in keV to compute emission for.
  Default: 0.025

kT_max

: The default maximum temperature in keV to compute emission for.
  Default: 64.0

max_density

: The maximum density of the cells or particles to use when generating
  photons. If a float, the units are assumed to be g/cm\*\*3.
  Default: None, meaning no maximum density.

min_entropy

: The minimum entropy of the cells or particles to use when generating
  photons. If a float, the units are assumed to be keV\*cm\*\*2.
  Default: None, meaning no minimum entropy.

var_elem

: Elements that should be allowed to vary freely from the single abundance
  parameter. Each dictionary value, specified by the abundance symbol,
  corresponds to the abundance of that symbol. If a float, it is understood
  to be constant and in solar units. If a string or tuple of strings, it is
  assumed to be a spatially varying field. Not yet available for "cloudy".
  Default: None

method

: The method used to generate the photon energies from the spectrum:
  "invert_cdf": Invert the cumulative distribution function of the spectrum.
  "accept_reject": Acceptance-rejection method using the spectrum.
  The first method should be sufficient for most cases.

thermal_broad

: Whether the spectral lines should be thermally
  broadened. Only available for "apec" or "spex". Default: True

model_root

: The directory root where the model files are stored. If not provided,
  a default location known to pyXSIM is used.

model_vers

: The version identifier string for the model files, e.g.
  "2.0.2", if supported by the model. Currently only supported by
  "apec", "spex", and "cloudy". Default depends on the model being used.
  If "cloudy", the options are:
  "4_lo": Tables computed from Cloudy using a continuum resolution
  of 0.1 with a range of 0.05 to 10 keV.
  "4_hi": Tables computed from Cloudy using enhanced continuum
  resolution of 0.025 with a range of 0.05 to 10 keV. Excellent
  energy resolution, but may be expensive to evaluate. Default for
  "cloudy" is "4_lo".

nolines

: Turn off lines entirely for generating emission. Only available
  for "apec" or "spex". Default: False

abund_table

: The abundance table to be used for solar abundances.
  Either a string corresponding to a built-in table or an array
  of 30 floats corresponding to the abundances of each element
  relative to the abundance of H. Not yet available for the "cloudy",
  CIE model, which always uses "feld". Otherwise, default is "angr".
  Built-in options are:
  "angr" : from Anders E. & Grevesse N. (1989, Geochimica et
  Cosmochimica Acta 53, 197)
  "aspl" : from Asplund M., Grevesse N., Sauval A.J. & Scott
  P. (2009, ARAA, 47, 481)
  "wilm" : from Wilms, Allen & McCray (2000, ApJ 542, 914
  except for elements not listed which are given zero abundance)
  "lodd" : from Lodders, K (2003, ApJ 591, 1220)
  "feld" : from Feldman U. (1992, Physica Scripta, 46, 202)
  "cl17.03" : the abundance table used in Cloudy v17.03.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically, will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers,
  such as for a test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> source_model = CIESourceModel("apec", 0.1, 10.0, 10000,
...                               ("gas", "metallicity"))
```

classes.name: pyxsim.source_models.thermal_sources.NEISourceModel
documentation:
Initialize a source model from a thermal spectrum, using the
APEC NEI tables from <https://www.atomdb.org>. Note that for this
class a set of specific element fields must be supplied. This should
really only be used with simulation data which include the appropriate
NEI calculations.

# Parameters

emin

: The minimum energy for the spectrum in keV.

emax

: The maximum energy for the spectrum in keV.

nbins

: The number of channels in the spectrum.

var_elem

: Abundances of elements. Each dictionary value, specified by the abundance
  symbol, corresponds to the abundance of that symbol. If a float, it is
  understood to be constant and in solar units. If a string or tuple of
  strings, it is assumed to be a spatially varying field.

binscale

: The scale of the energy binning: "linear" or "log".
  Default: "linear"

temperature_field

: The yt temperature field to use for the thermal modeling. Must have
  units of Kelvin. Default: ("gas","temperature")

emission_measure_field

: The yt emission measure field to use for the thermal modeling. Must
  have units of cm^-3. Default: ("gas","emission_measure")

h_fraction

: The hydrogen mass fraction. If a float, assumes a constant mass
  fraction of hydrogen throughout. If a string or tuple of strings,
  is taken to be the name of the hydrogen fraction field. Default is
  whatever value is appropriate for the chosen abundance table.

kT_min

: The default minimum temperature in keV to compute emission for.
  Default: 0.025

kT_max

: The default maximum temperature in keV to compute emission for.
  Default: 64.0

max_density

: The maximum density of the cells or particles to use when generating
  photons. If a float, the units are assumed to be g/cm\*\*3.
  Default: None, meaning no maximum density.

min_entropy

: The minimum entropy of the cells or particles to use when generating
  photons. If a float, the units are assumed to be keV\*cm\*\*2.
  Default: None, meaning no minimum entropy.

method

: The method used to generate the photon energies from the spectrum:
  "invert_cdf": Invert the cumulative distribution function of the spectrum.
  "accept_reject": Acceptance-rejection method using the spectrum.
  The first method should be sufficient for most cases.

thermal_broad

: Whether or not the spectral lines should be thermally
  broadened. Default: True

model_root

: The directory root where the model files are stored. If not provided,
  a default location known to pyXSIM is used.

model_vers

: The version identifier string for the model files, e.g.
  "2.0.2". Default is "3.0.9".

nolines

: Turn off lines entirely for generating emission.
  Default: False

abund_table

: The abundance table to be used for solar abundances.
  Either a string corresponding to a built-in table or an array
  of 30 floats corresponding to the abundances of each element
  relative to the abundance of H. Default is "angr".
  Built-in options are:
  "angr" : from Anders E. & Grevesse N. (1989, Geochimica et
  Cosmochimica Acta 53, 197)
  "aspl" : from Asplund M., Grevesse N., Sauval A.J. & Scott
  P. (2009, ARAA, 47, 481)
  "wilm" : from Wilms, Allen & McCray (2000, ApJ 542, 914
  except for elements not listed which are given zero abundance)
  "lodd" : from Lodders, K (2003, ApJ 591, 1220)
  "feld" : from Feldman U. (Physica Scripta, 46, 202)
  "cl17.03" : the abundance table used in Cloudy v17.03.

prng

: A pseudo-random number generator. Typically will only be specified
  if you have a reason to generate the same set of random numbers,
  such as for a test. Default is to use the {mod}`numpy.random` module.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> var_elem = {"H^1": ("flash", "h   "),
>>>             "He^0": ("flash", "he  "),
>>>             "He^1": ("flash", "he1 "),
>>>             "He^2": ("flash", "he2 "),
>>>             "O^0": ("flash", "o   "),
>>>             "O^1": ("flash", "o1  "),
>>>             "O^2": ("flash", "o2  "),
>>>             "O^3": ("flash", "o3  "),
>>>             "O^4": ("flash", "o4  "),
>>>             "O^5": ("flash", "o5  "),
>>>             "O^6": ("flash", "o6  "),
>>>             "O^7": ("flash", "o7  "),
>>>             "O^8": ("flash", "o8  ")
>>>            }
>>> source_model = NEISourceModel(0.1, 10.0, 10000, var_elem)
```
